schools.summary: Gill: these were the princes of the families of Gilead, of the tribe of Manasseh, whose grandson Gilead was.
Henry: the humble address the heads of Manasseh made to Moses and the princes, on occasion of the late order concerning the daughters.

themes: the chief fathers of the families of the children of Gilead... came near, and spake before Moses
the heads of Manasseh's humble appeal
seeking redress by lawful petition

schools.summary: Gill: one spoke in the name of the rest.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD commanded the land given by lot, and the inheritance of Zelophehad given to his daughters.

themes: The LORD commanded my lord to give the land... by lot... and... the inheritance of Zelophehad... unto his daughters
owning both former commands reverently
seeking how the grant might stand

schools.summary: Gill: the supposition was not unreasonable, and perhaps likely, from the application of young men of other tribes to them; the inheritance would be added to the tribe into which they married, and taken from their own.

themes: if they be married to any of the sons of the other tribes... their inheritance be taken from... our fathers
the heiresses' marriage moving the land
a real question of the tribal division

schools.summary: Gill: the jubilee, when inheritances returned to their original owners, would here be of no service but rather the contrary, fixing the daughters' land in another tribe.

themes: when the jubilee... shall be, then shall their inheritance be put unto... the tribe whereunto they are received
the jubilee failing to restore land passed by marriage
a special remedy needed

schools.summary: Gill: the law concerned all the tribes of Israel, not Manasseh only.
Keil & Delitzsch: Moses declared that what they had affirmed was right.

themes: The tribe of the sons of Joseph hath said well
God owning the justice of the appeal
a just law given to bind all the tribes

schools.summary: Gill: the Targum limits this order to the daughters of Zelophehad, or to what might happen before the land was divided; they might marry to whom they thought best, only within the tribe of their father.

themes: Let them marry to whom they think best; only to the family of the tribe of their father shall they marry
liberty of choice joined with order
the inheritance secured without robbing consent

schools.summary: Gill: this shows the law concerned all the tribes, that none's inheritance should remove to another; every Israelite was to keep to the inheritance of his fathers' tribe.

themes: So shall not the inheritance of the children of Israel remove from tribe to tribe
each keeping his fathers' portion
the unchanging inheritance pointing beyond (1 Pet. 1:4)

schools.summary: Gill: the same law that gave Zelophehad's daughters their right gave every daughter where there were no sons a right to inherit, and every such heiress was bound by this rule.
Clarke: every daughter possessing an inheritance was so bound.

themes: every daughter, that possesseth an inheritance... shall be wife unto one of the family of the tribe of her father
the case made a general statute
daughters' right established, every tribe secured

schools.summary: Gill: the jubilee alone did not sufficiently secure this without this law, as the case showed; every tribe was to keep to its own inheritance.

themes: Neither shall the inheritance remove from one tribe to another tribe... keep himself to his own inheritance
the great principle sealed
order and stability through the generations

schools.summary: Gill: they married into the family of their father's tribe, according to the account that follows; their obedience was exact to the command.

themes: Even as the LORD commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad
the daughters' ready, exact obedience
the right use of a granted right

schools.summary: Gill: the names are the same as before, the order a little varied — according to their marriage (Aben Ezra), or to show they were equal to one another (Jarchi); they married their father's brothers' sons.

themes: Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah... were married unto their father's brothers' sons
the five daughters married within the family
the faithful women remembered by name

schools.summary: Gill: they married into the family of the Hepherites, of which they were; their marrying into families (not one family) shows their cousins were not all sons of one man; their inheritance remained in the tribe of their father.

themes: they were married into the families of the sons of Manasseh... their inheritance remained in the tribe of... their father
the design of the law fulfilled
every claim met, the portion kept whole

schools.summary: Gill: these are the judicial laws concerning the division of the land, inheritances, and the cities of refuge, commanded by the hand of Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, closing the book.

themes: These are the commandments and the judgments, which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses... in the plains of Moab
the close of the book of Numbers
the people poised at the edge of the inheritance
